content.content:
- content: 'Record only observed case data: managed-account identifier, organization
    identifier,

    directory link reference, directory user identifier, application assignment status,

    synchronized group membership, latest provisioning event, and affected products.

    The case note is not a substitute for vendor policy when choosing the recovery
    branch.

    '
  metadata: {}
  rank: 1
  title: "SaaS Administration Case Notes \u2014 Directory-Linked Access Changes"
  url: null
- content: 'This service-catalog entry provides only the internal destination, request
    codes,

    and intake fields for Atlassian access-restoration work. It does not define the

    product-policy conditions that select local lifecycle handling, provisioned

    application-access restoration, or directory-link correction. Select a request
    code

    only after the applicable Atlassian product guidance and current records have
    already

    established the recovery control plane.


    Queue: Identity Operations / Application Access


    Service endpoint:

    https://internal.it.example.com/identity/atlassian-access-restoration


    Available request codes:

    - local_managed_account_restore

    - directory_application_access_restore

    - directory_link_review


    These codes are routing labels, not policy rules. The catalog does not establish
    how

    an active directory link, an available Reactivate account control, or a missing

    assignment maps to a request code.


    For directory_application_access_restore, include:

    - target_user_identifier

    - workspace_or_org_identifier

    - request_type

    - identity_provider

    - directory_user_identifier

    - application_name

    - application_assignment_status

    - group_name

    - group_membership_status

    - last_provisioning_event

    - requested_products

    - request_summary


    Do not include passwords, MFA codes, recovery codes, SCIM bearer tokens, API keys,

    OAuth secrets, or identity-provider administrator credentials.

    '
  metadata: {}
  rank: 2
  title: "Identity Operations Service Catalog \u2014 Atlassian Access Restoration"
  url: null
- content: 'The legacy vendor-support queue accepts locally managed Atlassian account
    cases after

    the applicable product guidance establishes that no active provisioning directory

    controls the relevant lifecycle or access state. This entry does not define the
    route

    for an account with an active external-directory link.

    '
  metadata: {}
  rank: 3
  title: Legacy Vendor Support Note for Non-Provisioned Accounts
  url: null

args.query: site:support.atlassian.com Atlassian Cloud SCIM user deactivated group assignment restore access application assignment

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Understand user provisioning | Atlassian Support

  - url: https://support.atlassian.com/provisioning-users/docs/understand-user-provisioning/

  - source: Atlassian Support

  - domain: support.atlassian.com


  ## Content


  Understand user provisioning


  User provisioning connects an identity-provider directory to an Atlassian organization.

  Users, account attributes, application assignments, and selected groups can be synchronized

  from the external directory into Atlassian.


  Identity-provider records control provisioned users and groups


  After an Atlassian account is linked to an identity-provider user, supported account
  and

  group updates are made in the identity provider and synchronized to Atlassian. Groups

  provisioned from the identity provider are read-only in Atlassian Administration.
  Their

  membership cannot be durably changed from the Atlassian group screen.


  Application assignment and synchronized-group changes


  Synced groups can grant Jira and Confluence application access. Removing a user
  from an

  identity-provider application assignment or from a synchronized access group can
  remove the

  corresponding Atlassian application access during provisioning.


  When application access was removed because the identity-provider application assignment
  or

  a synchronized-group membership was removed, restore that missing assignment or
  membership

  in the connected identity provider. After the identity-provider record is corrected,
  allow

  the next provisioning cycle to synchronize the record to Atlassian, or use a supported
  sync

  action to refresh the corrected record.


  If the provisioning link remains active and the identity-provider record remains

  unassigned, later synchronization continues to represent that upstream assignment
  and group

  state in Atlassian.


  Distinguish assignment restoration from link correction


  Use assignment or group restoration when the directory link is valid but the application

  assignment or synchronized membership is missing. Directory-link correction is a
  different

  procedure used when the link itself is incorrect or intentionally being retired.
  Do not

  unlink provisioning merely to bypass a valid identity-provider application-access
  state.


  Supported lifecycle operations


  Provisioning supports creating, linking, updating, activating, deactivating, and
  deleting

  user accounts from the identity provider. It also synchronizes supported group membership

  changes. Administrators should use the connected identity provider for records governed
  by

  the active provisioning connection.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Understand user provisioning | Atlassian Support
url: https://support.atlassian.com/provisioning-users/docs/understand-user-provisioning/

args.reason: File the authorized directory application-access restoration request because the active SCIM link is valid but the Okta Atlassian Cloud assignment and synchronized access-group membership were removed.

data.request_summary: Restore the user's Atlassian Cloud application assignment and membership in the synchronized atlassian-cloud-users group in Okta, then allow or trigger the next SCIM provisioning sync. The Atlassian directory link DIR-LINK-OKTA-7F41 is active and valid; access was removed after a group unassignment at 2026-07-06T13:18:00-07:00. Do not unlink the provisioning connection or use a local managed-account restore as a bypass.

args.reason: File the authorized directory application-access restoration request because the active SCIM link is valid but the Okta Atlassian Cloud assignment and synchronized access-group membership were removed.

data.request_summary: Restore the user's Atlassian Cloud application assignment and membership in the synchronized atlassian-cloud-users group in Okta, then allow or trigger the next SCIM provisioning sync. The Atlassian directory link DIR-LINK-OKTA-7F41 is active and valid; access was removed after a group unassignment at 2026-07-06T13:18:00-07:00. Do not unlink the provisioning connection or use a local managed-account restore as a bypass.

content.content:
The correct recovery path is **directory application-access restoration**, not a local Atlassian account reactivation.

Findings:
- The account is organization-managed through an active **Okta SCIM** connection.
- Jira and Confluence access was removed after the user was unassigned from the **Atlassian Cloud** Okta application and removed from the synchronized `atlassian-cloud-users` group.
- The Atlassian directory link remains valid.
- Atlassian guidance indicates the assignment/group membership must be restored in the identity provider, followed by SCIM synchronization. Unlinking provisioning or using the local Reactivate control would not be appropriate.

I filed the restoration request successfully with Identity Operations:

- **Request type:** `directory_application_access_restore`
- **User:** `managed-account-7f41`
- **Products:** Jira and Confluence
- **Provider:** Okta
- **Directory user:** `00u-atl-7f41`
- **Request destination:** Identity Operations / Application Access

The request asks them to restore the Okta application assignment and group membership, then allow or trigger the next SCIM sync.

final_answer:
The correct recovery path is **directory application-access restoration**, not a local Atlassian account reactivation.

Findings:
- The account is organization-managed through an active **Okta SCIM** connection.
- Jira and Confluence access was removed after the user was unassigned from the **Atlassian Cloud** Okta application and removed from the synchronized `atlassian-cloud-users` group.
- The Atlassian directory link remains valid.
- Atlassian guidance indicates the assignment/group membership must be restored in the identity provider, followed by SCIM synchronization. Unlinking provisioning or using the local Reactivate control would not be appropriate.

I filed the restoration request successfully with Identity Operations:

- **Request type:** `directory_application_access_restore`
- **User:** `managed-account-7f41`
- **Products:** Jira and Confluence
- **Provider:** Okta
- **Directory user:** `00u-atl-7f41`
- **Request destination:** Identity Operations / Application Access

The request asks them to restore the Okta application assignment and group membership, then allow or trigger the next SCIM sync.
